channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How can AI win the trust of doctors and nurses?
item.description: In August 2023, the American Medical Association surveyed over 1,000 physicians about their sentiments toward AI. The results, published at the end of that year, painted a nuanced picture.
item.dc:creator: Abdel Mahmoud, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia could be about to knock Apple off the top spot for most valuable company
item.description: After a brief moment in pole position, Nvidia is set to overtake Apple and become the world’s most valuable company.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: SUSE Edge upgrade targets Kubernetes and Linux at the edge
item.description: Open source and Linux platform vendor SUSE is looking to help organizations solve some of the complexity and challenges of edge computing with the company’s SUSE Edge 3.1 release, announced today.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Roku's new smart home features will make your TV the star of your smart home
item.description: Why watch TV when Roku's new smart home security features let you live out your dreams of becoming a security guard
item.dc:creator: josephine.watson@futurenet.com (Josephine Watson), TechRadar

item.title: DJI’s new dual-camera Air 3S drone could be the ultimate all-rounder for aerial photographers
item.description: DJI's new Air 3S drone gets upgraded dual cameras and LiDAR object sensing, making it one of the best all-rounder drones for aerial photography.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Corsair upgrades high-end gaming headset with Virtuoso Max Wireless
item.description: Reportedly priced at $329.99, the headset features active noise cancellation, RGB lighting accents and Dolby Atmos support
item.dc:creator: Gieson Cacho, The Mercury News

item.title: The world’s best camera phone is tipped to get even more powerful, thanks to this new 200MP zoom lens
item.description: The Xiaomi 15 Ultra is tipped to upgrade its long-range periscope camera, one of four housed in its industry-leading system
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft’s AI research VP joins OpenAI amid fight for top AI talent
item.description: Sebastien Bubeck, Microsoft’s vice president of GenAI research, is leaving the company to join OpenAI, the maker of ChatGPT.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google bets on nuclear power to drive AI expansion
item.description: Google has signed its first corporate deal to purchase power from multiple small modular reactors (SMRs) to meet the energy needs of its AI systems, marking a key step as AI companies shift toward nuclear power.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Copemar, en busca de la agilidad empresarial, avanza en su plan de digitalización
item.description: La empresa familiar dedicada en cuerpo y alma a la extracción, transformación y distribución de productos marinos, Copemar, da un paso adelante en su plan de transformación digital.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Biden admin to provide $750 million to North Carolina-based Wolfspeed for advanced computer chips
item.description: The Biden-Harris administration announced plans to provide up to $750 million in direct funding to Wolfspeed, with the money supporting its new silicon carbide factory in North Carolina that makes the wafers used in advanced computer chips and its factory in Marcy, New York.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: At the mercy of social media
item.description: I like Instagram as much as the next guy. I check in daily on my carefully curated feed of folks I follow and then work hard not to doomscroll the day away. One of my favorite follows is an adorable Corgi (aren’t they all adorable?) named Parsnip.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Revolutionizing Industry: How AI is Transforming the Manufacturing Sector
item.description: Discover how artificial intelligence is revolutionizing the manufacturing sector, increasing productivity and competitiveness.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The world’s space agencies are meeting in Milan. Here’s what’s on the agenda
item.description: The world’s space agencies are meeting in Milan this week as geopolitical rivalry fuels a new global race in Earth’s orbit and on the moon, with heavy involvement from a private sector toiling to keep pace with Elon Musk’s dominant SpaceX.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Johnson calls Kids Online Safety Act 'very problematic'
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) recently revealed he likes the concept of the Kids Online Safety Act (KOSA), though the details of the Senate-passed version are “very problematic,” underscoring the uncertain future the bill faces in the lower chamber.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Adobe aims to teach AI, online content skills to 30 million
item.description: A new program from Adobe aims to teach 30 million people AI, content creation, and marketing skills by 2030. It’s an extension of the Adobe Digital Academy, which already trains people for new careers in fields like digital marketing, user experience design, and data science.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Apple accused of restricting workers' Slack, social media use
item.description: The National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) filed a complaint against Apple on Thursday, accusing the iPhone maker of improperly restricting workers’ use of Slack and other social media.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: One Tech Tip: Here’s what you need to do before and after your phone is stolen or lost
item.description: There are things you can do to make it less painful if your phone is stolen. Because some of these features are more technical in nature, people often overlook them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Kenya moves 50 elephants to a larger park, says it's a sign poaching is low
item.description: Kenya is moving 50 elephants from a national reserve to a larger park after overpopulation overwhelmed the ecosystem, in what has been hailed as a conservation success.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Komu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Adobe makes GenStudio app generally available
item.description: Adobe’s GenStudio content supply chain platform is now generally available, with the ability to publish content directly to social media channels such as Instagram, Snap and TikTok coming soon.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 50 well-preserved Viking Age skeletons unearthed in Denmark
item.description: Archaeologists in Denmark have unearthed a Viking Age burial ground containing some 50 “exceptionally well-preserved” skeletons.
item.dc:creator: James Brooks, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How do we fund open source?
item.description: The world has come to rely upon the free work of millions of skilled software developers—the maintainers of free open-source software (FOSS) projects. But the world hasn’t given them a tip.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to leverage APIs for IT-enabled information capability
item.description: In today’s rapidly evolving digital landscape, businesses face constant pressure to adapt or risk being left behind. The strategic use of IT-enabled information management capability (IMC) is no longer just an advantage — it’s a necessity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Email Tool Revolution: Introducing PodSnacks - A Game-Changing Service for Podcast Enthusiasts
item.description: Discover the innovative service, PodSnacks, that uses AI to generate summaries of your favorite podcasts and sends them directly to your email inbox.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Musk's Starlink Internet free for hurricane victims through 2024
item.description: Following criticism, the company issued a new offer that extends the free service through the end of the year for victims of both hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: How to use Passwords, Apple iOS 18’s new iPhone password management app
item.description: Apple iOS 18, launched in September, is one of the most important updates to the iPhone’s operating system in the device’s history. Why? Because it lays the groundwork for Apple Intelligence, Apple’s artificial intelligence platform, which will power the phone in the years ahead.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Fake Jennifer Aniston bikini body ad
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Opinion: Musk’s courtship with MAGA has driven X into a misinformation abyss
item.description: If X is a town square, it's inundated with an uncontrolled, right-wing mob. And Musk is their cheering benefactor.
item.dc:creator: Lorraine Ali, The Mercury News

item.title: AMD unveils new generation of Epyc, Instinct chips
item.description: AMD announced its latest AI and high-performance computing processors at its Advancing AI event in San Francisco, including the fifth generation of its Epyc server processors and AMD Instinct MI325X accelerators.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: Reports
item.description: "Services are currently stopped to upgrade internal systems," Kahle wrote in a Thursday update. "We are working to restore services as quickly and safely as possible. Sorry for this disruption.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane’s impact on semiconductor industry remains a threat
item.description: One of two plants that mine high-purity quartz and silica sand needed for the production of semiconductors and other high-tech hardware has reopened operations after being shut down for more than a week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Strong Solar Radiation Storm Hits Earth - Geomagnetic Storm Expected
item.description: Earth hit by strong solar radiation storm, geomagnetic storm expected due to X1.8 solar flare and CME
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft unveils health care AI tools
item.description: Microsoft unveiled several new artificial intelligence (AI) tools on Thursday aiming to support health care organizations through medical imaging models, health care agent services and an AI-driven workflow solution for nurses.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, The Hill

item.title: AI tracker charts growth in deepfakes ahead of election
item.description: The German Marshall Fund has launched a tool to track deepfakes created by artificial intelligence (AI) that are targeting elections and circulating in the U.S. and across the globe. The tool comes in a historic year for elections.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity
item.description: Various industries are still dealing with the consequences of the July 2024 technology outage which led to a "blue screen of death" and extreme disruptions. The post Effective EDR: Balancing testing rigor and velocity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk steps up Starlink access as Hurricane Milton approaches Florida
item.description: SpaceX and CEO Elon Musk ramped up the rollout of Starlink's direct-to-cell service for areas expected to be hit Wednesday by Hurricane Milton, marking the latest assistance efforts by the aerospace company.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: iPhone update allows users to text for help when there's no cell service
item.description: The new update allows users to connect their phones to satellite, as long its an iPhone 14 or later model running iOS18.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, NewsNation
